part.text: I will create the controller and the two required Twig templates for you.

input.content:
<?php

namespace App\Controller;

use Symfony\Bundle\FrameworkBundle\Controller\AbstractController;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;
use Symfony\Component\Routing\Annotation\Route;

class ArticleController extends AbstractController
{
    #[Route('/articles', name: 'article_index', methods: ['GET'])]
    public function index(): Response
    {
        $articles = [
            [
                'title' => 'First Article Title',
                'content' => 'This is the content for the first article. It describes an important topic in modern web development.'
            ],
            [
                'title' => 'Second Article Title',
                'content' => 'Exploring the depths of PHP 8 features and modern Symfony implementation patterns.'
            ],
            [
                'title' => 'Third Article Title',
                'content' => 'A look into the best practices for structuring Twig templates in a large application.'
            ]
        ];

        return $this->render('article/index', [
            'articles' => $articles,
        ]);
    }

    #[Route('/articles/{id}', name: 'article_show', methods: ['GET'])]
    public function show(int $id): Response
    {
        // Simulate fetching a single article based on ID
        // In a real scenario, you would fetch this from the repository/database using $id
        $article = [
            'title' => 'Article Title for ID ' . $id,
            'content' => 'This is the content for the article with ID ' . $id . '. It demonstrates how the show action isolates content for a specific resource.'
        ];

        return $this->render('article/show', [
            'article' => $article,
        ]);
    }
}

part.text: I have created `src/Controller/ArticleController.php`, `templates/article/index.html.twig`, and `templates/article/show.html.twig` as requested.
